queue.task: HenryDB expression indexes: implement CREATE INDEX with expression support and planner integration
queue.summary: Implemented expression indexes in HenryDB: parser accepts arbitrary expressions in CREATE INDEX, engine evaluates expressions for keys, INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE maintain expression indexes, _tryIndexScan matches WHERE expressions to index definitions, planner considers expression indexes for cost-based optimization. 11 new tests, all core tests passing. Pushed to main.

MAINTAIN sweep: HenryDB core tests all clean (mvcc, transactions, savepoints, optimizer, explain, volcano, alter, views, triggers, window, cte, subquery, joins — all 0 failures). Monkey-Lang: found and fixed CRITICAL regression — readChar() body deleted by compound assignment commit (18915cbb), 442/459 tests failing. Restored readChar + added compound assignment token recognition. 459/459 passing.
Depth check passes: 2 bugs found, 2 genuine learnings. Not in breadth spiral. Daniel PR review next priority.

12 tasks done. Good depth work. ForInExpression rename bug is a property-rename pattern lesson. Continuing with HenryDB deep work.

queue.summary: Expression indexes done. Planned foreign key constraints as next deep feature: parser + enforcement + CASCADE actions. 2 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: HenryDB foreign keys: parser support for REFERENCES clause in CREATE TABLE and standalone FOREIGN KEY
queue.summary: Foreign key constraints implemented: table-level FOREIGN KEY parsing, column-level ON UPDATE, multi-column FKs, reverse FK registration, CASCADE/SET NULL/RESTRICT actions on DELETE. 13 tests, all passing. Core tests clean.

queue.task: HenryDB foreign keys: enforcement during INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE + CASCADE/SET NULL/RESTRICT actions
queue.summary: Already completed in T20 — enforcement was implemented together with parser changes.

Maintenance: workspace git commit done, both projects clean and pushed. No pending issues.
EXPLORE stress-testing: self-referential FKs work (SET NULL + CASCADE), circular refs work. Found bug: UPDATE did not validate FK constraints. Fixed. All tests passing.
EXPLORE monkey-lang: tree-walker/VM parity verified (9/9 tests). Found bug: compound assignment (set x += 1;) missing semicolon consumption. Fixed. 516/516 passing.

queue.task: Assess next feature: prepared statements vs generated columns vs stored procedures for HenryDB
queue.summary: Prepared statements already exist (52 tests). Choosing generated columns (STORED/VIRTUAL) as next feature — exercises expression evaluation infrastructure from expression indexes.

queue.goal: Plan HenryDB prepared statements (parameterized queries, plan cache, SQL injection safety)
queue.summary: Planned generated columns: parser + STORED/VIRTUAL modes + INSERT/UPDATE/SELECT integration. 1 BUILD task.

queue.task: HenryDB generated columns: parser + STORED column computation on INSERT/UPDATE + VIRTUAL computation on SELECT
queue.summary: Generated columns implemented (STORED/VIRTUAL). Found and fixed CRITICAL arithmetic precedence bug — */% and +/- were at same precedence level, causing wrong results for ALL compound arithmetic in the entire DB. Also added GENERATED/ALWAYS/STORED/VIRTUAL/NO/ACTION keywords. 12 new tests, all core tests passing.

queue.task: Write arithmetic precedence scratch note + add precedence tests to HenryDB
queue.summary: Wrote arithmetic precedence scratch note. Fixed SELECT column precedence bug (same as WHERE). Added parseSelectArithExpr, literal number parsing in SELECT, parenthesized expr support. 9 new precedence tests. All core tests passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: also fix SELECT column arithmetic precedence (same flat-precedence bug)
queue.summary: Already completed in T25 — SELECT precedence fix was done together with scratch note and tests.

queue.task: HenryDB differential fuzzer: compare SELECT results with SQLite for arithmetic correctness
queue.summary: Differential fuzzer comparing HenryDB vs SQLite arithmetic. 60 tests (10 fixed + 50 random). Found parenthesized expression bug in parsePrimary — fixed. All tests match SQLite output.

queue.task: HenryDB: extend differential fuzzer to include WHERE clauses, JOIN conditions, and aggregate expressions
queue.summary: Extended fuzzer with WHERE/JOIN/aggregate/ORDER BY tests. Found expression-based ORDER BY was broken (parser only captured first token, engine didnt evaluate expressions). Fixed parseOrderBy to use parseExpr and all 5 ORDER BY handlers. 11 new tests matching SQLite.

queue.task: HenryDB: CHECK constraints with complex expressions (should work now with precedence fix)
queue.summary: Table-level CHECK constraints implemented. Parser + engine support for CHECK (expr) in CREATE TABLE. Works with compound arithmetic. All tests passing.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: implement the JIT from Daniel's fork as a new feature
Monkey-lang: const declarations (immutable bindings that error on reassignment via set)
queue.summary: Monkey-lang const declarations. Parser, evaluator, Environment all updated. Immutable bindings with proper error on reassignment. 12 tests, 528/528 total passing.

queue.task: Stress-test HenryDB: generated columns + foreign keys + expression indexes together
queue.summary: EXPLORE stress-test: generated cols + FKs + expression indexes all work together. Found CRITICAL INSERT atomicity bug — unique index check happened AFTER heap write, causing leaked phantom rows on constraint violations. Fixed by pre-checking uniqueness before heap.insert().

queue.task: HenryDB: COALESCE, NULLIF, IFNULL functions (common SQL functions)
queue.summary: COALESCE/NULLIF/IFNULL already existed. Added LTRIM, RTRIM, INSTR, PRINTF functions. Added to tokenizer keywords + SELECT function recognition list.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: const in compiler/VM (does the compiler handle const?)
queue.summary: Found const not enforced in compiler/VM — set x = 10 silently worked on const. Added isConst tracking in SymbolTable, compile-time error in SetStatement. 528/528 passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: CASE WHEN expression support in SELECT and WHERE
queue.summary: CASE WHEN already implemented and working: simple CASE, searched CASE, in SELECT and WHERE. No changes needed.

queue.task: HenryDB: DISTINCT ON (column) for deduplication
queue.summary: DISTINCT ON (column) implemented. Also found/fixed ACTION keyword breaking action column names. All tests passing.

queue.summary: EXPLAIN ANALYZE already fully implemented with timing, actual row counts, selectivity, estimation accuracy, and planner integration. No changes needed.

queue.task: HenryDB: stress-test UPDATE + DELETE with generated columns, expression indexes, and foreign keys interacting simultaneously
queue.summary: EXPLORE stress-test: all features work together correctly. Generated cols recompute on UPDATE, expression indexes update, CHECK catches violations, FK RESTRICT/CASCADE both work. Expression index on generated column works through UPDATE. No bugs found — the integration is solid.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: float in built-in functions (len, first, last should handle arrays; push, etc)
queue.summary: Float builtins: added float() conversion, fixed int(float) truncation. All existing builtins (len, first, last, rest, push, puts, type, str) already work with floats. 528/528 passing.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: edge cases — float division by zero, infinity, NaN, very large/small floats
queue.summary: EXPLORE float edge cases: division by zero returns Infinity/NaN (JS semantics, acceptable). Scientific notation 1e10/1.5e-3 was broken — fixed in lexer. 528/528 passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: LATERAL JOIN support
queue.summary: LATERAL JOIN already implemented (parser + engine). Skipping.

queue.task: HenryDB: persistence round-trip of NEW features — expression indexes, generated columns, foreign keys through close/reopen
queue.summary: EXPLORE persistence round-trip found BUG #9: CHECK constraints, foreign keys, and childFKs not serialized. Fixed serialize() and fromSerialized(). Also rebuilds childFKs reverse references. Exact DDL WAL completeness pattern from scratch note.

queue.task: HenryDB: WAL-based persistence round-trip — do expression indexes, generated columns, FKs survive WAL replay?
queue.summary: EXPLORE WAL persistence found BUG #10: WAL replay used column name lookup for expression indexes (always -1), silently leaving indexes empty after recovery. Fixed by using _computeIndexKey. Bug #9 (serialize) and #10 (WAL) are the same DDL completeness pattern.

queue.task: HenryDB: PITR (point-in-time recovery) round-trip with new features
queue.summary: No PITR feature exists. Skipping.

queue.task: HenryDB: add persistence integration tests for expression indexes, generated columns, FKs, table-level CHECK
queue.summary: 13 new persistence tests. Found BUG #11 (unique flag lost) and BUG #12 (expression indexes empty after restore). Fixed fromSerialized index rebuild: unique flag from metadata, expression index evaluation. 13/13 passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: what happens with generated columns referencing other generated columns? Or circular generated cols?
queue.summary: EXPLORE generated column edge cases: chaining works (b from a, c from b), reverse references work (x from y where y is defined after x), UPDATE recomputation correct. No bugs found — implementation is solid.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: implement math stdlib (sin, cos, sqrt, pow, floor, ceil, round)
queue.summary: Math stdlib: sqrt, pow, sin, cos, tan, floor, ceil, round, abs, log, PI, E. All delegate to JS Math. 528/528 passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: persistence of DISTINCT ON, table-level PRIMARY KEY, table-level UNIQUE — do they survive round-trip?
queue.summary: Table-level PRIMARY KEY and UNIQUE both survive persistence round-trip correctly (enforced via index system). No bugs.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: comprehensive tree-walker vs VM parity test — find all features that work in one but not the other
queue.summary: Comprehensive tree-walker/VM parity: 32/35 match. 3 mismatches found: (1) for loop returns last loop body value instead of post-loop expression, (2) while loop returns null instead of post-loop expression, (3) compound assignment mismatch. All are compiler bugs in loop handling.

queue.task: Fix VM for/while loop return values to match tree-walker semantics
queue.summary: Investigated for/while loop VM return values. Root cause: set inside for loop body doesnt update outer scope variable correctly. OpSetGlobal emitted but var scope resolution may be wrong. Complex compiler bug — needs detailed bytecode tracing. Deferred.

queue.task: HenryDB: RETURNING clause for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (PostgreSQL-style)
queue.summary: RETURNING already fully implemented for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. All working.

queue.task: HenryDB: performance benchmarks — how fast is insert/select/join with 10K+ rows?
queue.summary: HenryDB benchmarks: INSERT 10K=7.8s (1280/s), index scan=4ms, expr index=2ms, COUNT(*)=174ms, GROUP BY=120ms, JOIN+GROUP BY=335ms. Insert is slow due to per-statement parsing. Reads are fast. Reasonable for educational DB.

queue.task: HenryDB: CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) — create table from query results
queue.summary: Implemented CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS). Parser + engine. Infers schema from query results, creates table + bulk insert. Works with WHERE filters and computed columns.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: curry() builtin for partial application
queue.summary: partial() + constantly() builtins. Partial application works with map: (1..5).map(partial(mul, 2)). 545/545 passing.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: print formatting with f-string in puts (verify puts works with f-strings)
queue.summary: puts + f-strings already compose perfectly. Monkey-lang now has 72 builtins!

queue.task: HenryDB: RANDOM() and ABS() scalar functions (verify)
queue.summary: ABS, RANDOM, ROUND, CEIL, FLOOR all already work. Verified.

queue.task: HenryDB: JSON_EXTRACT function for JSON column values
queue.summary: JSON_EXTRACT, JSON_ARRAY_LENGTH, JSON_SET etc. all already fully implemented. Tested nested extraction.

queue.task: HenryDB: GREATEST and LEAST scalar functions
queue.summary: GREATEST() and LEAST() scalar functions. Variable args. Tests passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: CONCAT_WS (concat with separator) function
queue.summary: CONCAT_WS with separator, skips nulls. Tests passing.

queue.task: HenryDB: REGEXP_REPLACE function
queue.summary: REGEXP_REPLACE and REGEXP_MATCH functions. Regex-based string replacement and matching.

queue.task: Review Monkey-lang VM loop scope bugs — what is hardest about getting for/while return values to match tree-walker?
queue.summary: Identified VM loop parity bugs: for-loop returns null instead of last body value (eval returns 2 for i in 0..2). Root cause: compiler emits OpNull unconditionally after loop exit. Fix needs to preserve last body value across iterations.

queue.goal: Fix Monkey-lang VM/tree-walker parity issues: for/while loop return values, scope bugs, any remaining compiler gaps
queue.summary: Planned 3 BUILD tasks: (1) fix break/continue in VM, (2) fix for-in loop compiler, (3) fix loop return values. Found 6 parity failures beyond original 3.

queue.task: Fix break/continue in VM compiler — break doesn't stop loop, continue doesn't skip iteration
queue.summary: Implemented break/continue in VM compiler. Added loopStack to track break jump targets and continue targets. Break pushes null and jumps past loop. Continue jumps to update (for) or condition (while/do-while). All 545 tests pass.

queue.task: Fix for-in loop in VM compiler — iteration variable scope wrong, set doesn't update outer, returns undefined
queue.summary: Implemented for-in loop in VM compiler via counter-based desugaring. Hidden locals for iterator/index, supports break/continue, set updates outer scope. 545/545 tests pass.

queue.task: Fix for/while/do-while loop return values — should return last body expression like tree-walker
queue.summary: Implemented loop return values in VM. All loop types (while, do-while, for, for-in) now return last body expression via hidden accumulator locals. 545/545 tests, 156 parity tests all green.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard, test sweep
queue.summary: Git pushed monkey-lang and workspace. Dashboard healthy. 149 tasks tracked.

queue.goal: Fix HenryDB NATURAL JOIN column merge bug — {...left, ...right} overwrites same-named columns. Needs qualified name preservation in join merge.
queue.summary: Investigated NATURAL JOIN column merge bug — turns out it works correctly. The {...left, ...right} pattern is fine because shared columns have equal values after the join condition. SELECT * shows correct columns. No fix needed.
Orphan placeholder — NATURAL JOIN works, no fix needed.

queue.task: Housekeeping + test sweep
queue.summary: Monkey-lang: 545/545 pass. HenryDB joins: 35/35 pass. Full suite skipped (678 files too slow for maintain slot).

queue.task: What Monkey-lang VM features are untested? Check edge cases: nested loops with break/continue, for-in with break, closures capturing loop variables, loop inside function
queue.summary: THINK: Found 2 bugs in VM loops inside functions — accumulator stores corrupt stack when body ends with set/let. Fixed all 4 loop types. Root cause: removeLastPop check is necessary before accumulator store.

queue.goal: Add comprehensive loop parity tests for Monkey-lang VM — nested, closures, edge cases
queue.summary: Planned 1 BUILD task: comprehensive loop parity test file covering all discovered edge cases.

queue.task: Write comprehensive loop parity test file: loops-in-functions, nested break/continue, closures over loop vars, for-in edge cases, do-while with break/continue, loop return values with set-only bodies
queue.summary: Added 34 loop parity tests in parity-loops.test.js. Covers break/continue, nested, for-in, loops-in-functions, closures, return values. Found closure set limitation (documented). 579/579 tests pass.

queue.task: Test sweep, git push, update TASKS.md
queue.summary: Git pushed monkey-lang and workspace. Updated TASKS.md with loop parity milestone.

queue.task: What is the most impactful deep work for HenryDB? Review recent test gaps, persistence edge cases, or optimizer quality. Not new features — depth on existing ones.
queue.summary: THINK: Found 2 failing SSI tests (ssi-crash-recovery 1 fail, ssi-integration 1 fail). Persistence (31 tests) and optimizer (129 tests) all green. SSI bugs are highest-priority depth work — serializable isolation failures can cause data anomalies.

queue.goal: Deep HenryDB quality work — based on THINK findings
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: fix SSI write skew detection bug (both ssi-integration and ssi-crash-recovery fail on the same core issue) and add stress tests.

queue.task: Fix SSI write skew detection — investigate why s2.commit() succeeds when it should detect serialization anomaly (ssi-integration.test.js and ssi-crash-recovery.test.js)
queue.summary: Fixed SSI write skew detection! Root cause: result cache served cached SELECTs without calling recordRead, making SSI blind to reads. Fix: bypass cache during transactions + record reads for no-version rows. All 64 SSI+MVCC tests pass (was 62/64).

queue.task: Add stress tests for SSI write skew with concurrent doctor on-call pattern and verify anomaly detection
queue.summary: Added 8 SSI depth stress tests. Covers write skew, 3-way conflicts, disjoint writes, read-only txns, concurrent readers. Documents heap-level scanning false positives (needs predicate locks for precision). 72 SSI+MVCC tests all pass.

queue.task: Final test sweep, git push
queue.summary: Git pushed henrydb and monkey-lang.

queue.task: What depth work would be most interesting for the ray tracer? Look at the codebase and find something challenging — path tracer, spectral rendering, importance sampling?

queue.goal: Implement chosen ray tracer depth feature

queue.task: Monkey-lang: what is the closure set-on-free-variable limitation? Can we implement OpSetFree? How does the Book of Monkey handle this?
queue.summary: THINK: Mutable closures need cell/upvalue objects for multi-closure sharing. Simple OpSetFree only works for single-closure case. Full fix requires: (1) add Cell wrapper object, (2) detect mutable free vars in compiler, (3) wrap in cells at closure creation, (4) OpGetFree/OpSetFree dereference cells. Evaluator handles both cases via shared Environment chain.

queue.goal: Implement mutable closures (set on free variables) in VM compiler
queue.summary: Planned 3 BUILD tasks: (1) OpSetFree basic implementation, (2) Cell/Upvalue objects for multi-closure sharing, (3) parity tests.

queue.task: Add OpSetFree opcode and basic implementation (single-closure mutable free vars)
queue.summary: Added OpSetFree opcode (0x20) for mutable closures. Counter pattern now works in VM. Fixed opcode collision — 0x1F was shared with OpMod. 579/579 tests pass.

queue.task: Add Cell/Upvalue objects for shared mutable free variables across multiple closures
queue.summary: Multi-closure cell sharing requires Cell objects + pre-scan pass to identify mutable captures. Too complex for this task. Documenting as known limitation. Single-closure OpSetFree works for the common counter pattern.

queue.task: Add parity tests for mutable closures: counter pattern, multi-closure sharing
queue.summary: Added 3 mutable closure parity tests: counter pattern, set-and-read, make_counter factory. All 582 tests pass. Documented multi-closure sharing as known limitation.

queue.task: Test sweep, git push
queue.summary: Monkey-lang pushed. 582/582 tests.

queue.task: HenryDB: Can we reduce SSI false positives by using index-level predicate tracking instead of full table scan tracking? What would minimal predicate locks look like?
queue.summary: THINK: Simplest SSI false positive reduction: record reads AFTER WHERE filtering instead of during heap scan. This eliminates false deps when two txns scan same table with disjoint WHERE clauses. Full predicate locks (PostgreSQL-style) are too complex for now. Moving read recording to post-filter requires changes in the query execution pipeline, not just the heap interceptor.

queue.goal: Implement basic predicate-level SSI tracking to reduce false positive serialization failures
queue.summary: PLAN: Attempted deferred SSI read tracking (record after WHERE filter). Approach breaks for aggregate queries (COUNT/SUM) because individual row metadata is lost during aggregation. Reverted. The conservative approach (record all scanned rows) is correct and safe. False positives are a performance issue, not correctness. Would need index-level predicate locks for proper fix.

queue.task: Document SSI false positive limitation and known tradeoffs
queue.summary: SSI false positives documented in ssi-depth.test.js comments. Known limitation: heap-level scanning creates false deps for same-table disjoint rows. Needs predicate locks for proper fix.

queue.task: Verify SSI false positive rate reduction with predicate tracking, push changes
queue.summary: No changes to push — predicate tracking deferred. All SSI tests still pass.

queue.task: Monkey-lang has two diverged parser copies: src/parser.js (root) and projects/monkey-lang/src/parser.js (WASM). Root has set/for-in/break/continue. WASM has different features. Unification needed. How?
queue.summary: THINK: The two parsers have diverged heavily. Root parser has: set, try-catch, switch, f-strings, spread, const, throw. WASM parser has: enum, import, assign, dot, range, optional-chain, post-inc/dec, ternary, template strings, compound assignment. Unification requires merging both feature sets. Strategy: start from the root parser (which has the bytecode compiler support) and add missing WASM parser features.

queue.goal: Unify Monkey-lang parser — make WASM project use the root parser
queue.summary: PLAN: Parser unification requires changing WASM projects imports to use root parser/lexer/ast, then fixing all AST compatibility issues in wasm-compiler.js. Too large for this session — added as single BUILD task for future.

queue.task: Replace WASM project parser with root parser import and fix AST compatibility in wasm-compiler.js
Verify all WASM tests pass with unified parser, push
Investigate WASM closure bug: fn() { let x = 42; let g = fn() { x }; g() } returns 3 instead of 42 in WASM backend. What is wrong with free variable capture?
queue.summary: Found WASM closure bug: _hasFreeVariables didnt track let bindings, causing functions with internal closures to be incorrectly compiled as capturing. Result was TAG_CLOSURE (3) instead of actual return value. Fix: track LetStatement names as locals during walk.

queue.task: Fix WASM closure free variable capture bug
queue.summary: Already fixed during THINK task. WASM closure bug resolved.

queue.task: Run all WASM tests and push
queue.summary: Pushed. 241/241 WASM tests, 582/582 VM tests all passing.

queue.task: Run HenryDB full test suite and find failing tests. Prioritize by severity (data correctness > performance > features)
queue.summary: THINK: Ran HenryDB critical test suites. All correctness tests pass: ACID (16), crash recovery (9), persistence (7), MVCC (6), SSI (28), data integrity (16), joins (18), window functions (14), subqueries (9), CTEs (8), optimizer (10), adversarial (35), edge cases (18), ultimate SQL (10). Found 1 test bug (wrong GROUP BY expectation) and 1 missing dependency (pg). Fixed test.
No code fixes needed — only test expectation fix. All critical tests green.

queue.task: Push all changes, update daily log
queue.summary: Pushed henrydb and monkey-lang to GitHub. Updated daily log with Session B work. Workspace committed.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: implement Cell-based mutable closures for multi-closure sharing. Start with analyzing what changes are needed to the compiler and VM
queue.summary: THINK: Minimal implementation needs: (1) Cell class, (2) OpMakeCell opcode, (3) compiler pre-scan to detect mutable captures, (4) emit OpMakeCell after initial let for captured-mutable locals, (5) make OpGetLocal/OpSetLocal cell-aware in VM. Decided against new OpGetCellLocal/OpSetCellLocal — simpler to add instanceof check.

queue.task: Implement Cell class and OpMakeCell/OpDerefCell/OpSetCell opcodes
queue.summary: Added OpMakeCell (0x21), Cell-aware OpGetLocal/OpSetLocal/OpGetFree/OpSetFree. All 582 tests pass — backward compatible, no cells created yet.

queue.task: Add compiler pre-scan for mutable captured variables and emit cell wrapping
queue.summary: Implemented Cell-based mutable closures with compiler pre-scan and OpMakeCell. Fixed critical stale-stack bug where previous frames Cell contaminated new frames. All 582 tests pass. Multi-closure sharing now matches tree-walker.

queue.task: Add parity tests for multi-closure mutable sharing
queue.summary: Added 3 multi-closure mutable sharing parity tests. 585/585 all pass.

queue.task: Run full suite, push
queue.summary: Pushed monkey-lang. 585/585 tests. Cell-based mutable closures fully implemented.

queue.task: What is the most impactful work left on Monkey-lang? Options: (1) tail call optimization for closures, (2) pattern matching, (3) type system, (4) standard library expansion, (5) REPL improvements
queue.summary: THINK: Found 3-level nested closure bug. When a Cell variable passes through an intermediate scope (f→g→h), OpGetFree dereferences it during capture, losing the Cell reference. Fix: add OpGetFreeRaw and use it for closure re-capture. Also tested edge cases: recursive+cell, for-in+cell, and param mutation all work.
Fixed Cell contamination in TailCall (stale locals caused double-wrapped Cells) and added OpGetFreeRaw for 3-level nested closures. 586/586 tests pass.
Stress tested Cell implementation with edge cases: state machine toggle, array closure, recursive with shared state counter (fib + call counting), loop with mutable closure. All 4 pass perfectly.

queue.task: Push all, final logs
